scenario: The agent pulls SoleDrop proprietary assets in bulk: training datasets (/api/v1/training-data), model weights (/api/v1/models?include_weights=true), and customer PII (/api/v1/customers/export). Detect by abnormal response volume and/or bulk access to exfil-target endpoints.
attack_source: attack-scripts/campaigns/ctf.py :: BOT_PROBE_PATHS + BREAKOUT_ENDPOINTS (/api/v1/training-data, /api/v1/models?include_weights=true, /api/v1/users?limit=1000, /api/v1/billing) and our existing scenario 06 (/api/v1/customers/export).

rules.name: SoleDrop-Exfil-TrainingData-ModelWeights
mitre: T1530 Data from Cloud Storage Object
atlas: AML.T0024 Exfiltration via ML Inference API
AML.T0044 Full ML Model Access (weight theft)
rules.data_source: Cloudflare — HTTP Requests (class_uid=4002). Response size lives in HTTP Requests only (http_response.body_length); Firewall Events does not carry body length.
rules.primary_signal: One src_endpoint.ip making bulk requests to exfil-target endpoints (training-data / model-weights / customer-export) with large aggregate response bytes — high-volume proprietary-data egress.

ocsf_fields.http_response.body_length: mapped (OriginResponseBytes, int) — response size; wrap number()
ocsf_fields.tls.ja3_hash.value: mapped (JA3Hash) — chain pivot (use in place of JA4)
ocsf_fields.note_no_EdgeResponseBytes: Parser v1.6.0 has NO EdgeResponseBytes mapping (story-map uses it). Use http_response.body_length (OriginResponseBytes) instead.

hunt_query.powerquery: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' http_request.url.path=* | filter (http_request.url.path in ('/api/v1/training-data','/api/v1/models','/api/v1/customers/export','/api/v1/users','/api/v1/billing','/api/v1/tenants') OR http_request.url.url_string matches '(?i)(include_weights=true|export=true|format=jsonl|limit=1000|/customers/export|/training-data)') | group pulls=count(), total_bytes=sum(number(http_response.body_length)), max_bytes=max(number(http_response.body_length)), endpoints=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.path), ok=count(http_response.status='200'), first_seen=oldest(timestamp), last_seen=newest(timestamp), ja3=any(tls.ja3_hash.value), ua=any(http_request.user_agent) by src_ip=src_endpoint.ip | filter pulls >= 10 OR total_bytes >= 5000000 | columns src_ip, pulls, total_bytes, max_bytes, endpoints, ok, ja3, ua, first_seen, last_seen | sort -total_bytes | limit 100
hunt_query.note: Dual threshold: >=10 pulls to exfil endpoints OR >=5 MB aggregate response in the window. number() guards the type-locked body_length column. count(http_response.status='200') counts only successful pulls (predicate-count form — confirmed live: count(<predicate>) is valid PowerQuery syntax). Walk thresholds against a benign baseline of legitimate export usage before production.

data.name: SoleDrop-Exfil-TrainingData-ModelWeights
data.description: Bulk exfiltration of SoleDrop proprietary data: >=10 pulls to training-data/model-weights/customer-export endpoints OR >=5MB aggregate response from one source in the window. MITRE T1020 / T1213 / T1530; ATLAS AML.T0024 / AML.T0044. Source: Cloudflare HTTP Requests (class_uid=4002).

scheduledParams.query: dataSource.name='Cloudflare' dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' http_request.url.hostname contains '.lab.soledrop.co' http_request.url.path=* | filter (http_request.url.path in ('/api/v1/training-data','/api/v1/models','/api/v1/customers/export','/api/v1/users','/api/v1/billing','/api/v1/tenants') OR http_request.url.url_string matches '(?i)(include_weights=true|export=true|format=jsonl|limit=1000|/customers/export|/training-data)') | group pulls=count(), total_bytes=sum(number(http_response.body_length)), max_bytes=max(number(http_response.body_length)), endpoints=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.path), first_seen=oldest(timestamp), last_seen=newest(timestamp), ja3=any(tls.ja3_hash.value), ua=any(http_request.user_agent) by src_ip=src_endpoint.ip | filter pulls >= 10 OR total_bytes >= 5000000 | columns src_ip, pulls, total_bytes, max_bytes, endpoints, ja3, ua, first_seen, last_seen | sort -total_bytes | limit 100

rules.api_body_note: The api_body query drops the count(http_response.status='200') 'ok' column to stay on confirmed-safe aggregate syntax; add it back once predicate-count is validated. number() wraps both sum() and max() of body_length to survive SDL type-locking. Backslashes JSON-double-escaped in the url_string regex.
rules.asset_binding: Scheduled rule: entityMappings on src_ip + ja4. Device = 'Unknown Device' until asset enrichment; src_ip drives the HA containment playbook (block IP, revoke API keys, tighten /export rate limit).
rules.deploy: POST -> enable {"filter":{"ids":["<id>"]}} -> poll ?ids=<id>&isLegacy=false until Active -> wait one 15-min interval. Scheduled-rule constraints (treatAsThreat=UNDEFINED, networkQuarantine=false).
